### Session 54acab78-d53f-41a6-955f-aa0bb18cac7e - 2026-07-10T19:47:28.722Z

- User reported an n8n workflow failure: Jobber OAuth2 credential returned a 401 authorization error with an invalid refresh token, preventing access to the Jobber API for a visits query scheduled between July 11–20
- Claude Code diagnosed the issue as a dead refresh token stored in the n8n credential (likely from Jobber's refresh token rotation mechanism) and noted that CLI-side auth still works
- Provided immediate fix: reconnect the credential in n8n's Credentials panel by approving the Jobber OAuth flow again and saving
- Identified root cause: Jobber's refresh token rotation policy makes tokens single-use; when multiple tools (n8n + local scripts) hold separate refresh tokens, rotation causes chain conflicts and token invalidation
- Recommended long-term solution: disable refresh token rotation in Jobber Developer Center, or create a second Jobber OAuth app exclusively for n8n to avoid shared credential chains
- Advised that if rotation stays enabled, the backfill run tonight could trigger token conflicts again when both n8n and the script attempt to refresh
- Requested confirmation after reconnecting so Claude could proceed with pulling the visit numbers

### Session 54acab78-d53f-41a6-955f-aa0bb18cac7e - 2026-07-10T20:21:53.544Z

- Spencer reported that his Java developer center refresh token rotation toggle was already turned off, yet the credential died again
- Claude Code investigated the notes workflow credential (`Jobber OAuth2 native`) and confirmed it was not replaced and ran via scripts today, not n8n, ruling it out as the cause
- Root cause identified: the rotation toggle policy change itself — the n8n credential was connected July 6 while rotation was still enabled, holding a single-use-generation refresh token from July 7; when rotation was turned off, Jobber invalidated all outstanding rotated tokens, leaving n8n's credential stale after 3 days of inactivity
- Scripts survived because they continuously refresh (always holding current tokens), while n8n's stored token sat idle and became invalid
- Solution provided: reconnect the n8n credential once (n8n → Credentials → "Jobber OAuth2 (native)" → Reconnect → approve popup → Save), then execute the workflow; with rotation now off, the new refresh token will be long-lived and reusable
- Fallback escalation noted: if the credential dies again, create a dedicated second Jobber app for n8n with isolated credentials, though this is not expected to be necessary
- Requested Spencer report back after the run so Claude can pull the numbers

### Session 7936f00c-c618-446a-8ffb-91af96e234d0 - 2026-07-10T21:07:05.435Z

- User raised two concerns about the Jobber-notes automation: (1) how to handle misspellings and off-format notes that don't fit the parser, and (2) whether switching to a job form with dropdowns would be better than freeform notes for data capture and reporting.

- Claude Code encountered a Jobber local auth failure (HTTP 401 on token refresh—stale cached token), blocking local script verification; n8n workflow remains unaffected due to separate credentials.

- Analyzed note-parser reliability across 442 real notes: activity captures 96%, trap inventory 100%, but catch counts only 74% and next-action 76%—non-matching lines fail silently with no alerting.

- Identified the original spec gap: missing exceptions lane (daily review queue) that would catch nonconforming notes before data loss occurs, with optional AI fallback to handle misspellings.

- Separated the form question into two: reporting (an output problem, solvable with or without forms via per-visit database rows) and input method (a genuine trade-off between freeform speed and form-dropdown error-proofing).

- Noted a critical unknown blocker: whether Jobber's API can read job form submission responses programmatically; if not, form data becomes inaccessible to automation.

- Recommended: add exceptions lane and per-visit database output before switching inputs; watch exception rate for 2 weeks to validate notes approach; verify form-API readability first if forms are considered; create a one-page tech cheat sheet regardless.

- Identified blocker: local Jobber auth is dead (401 error from earlier); re-authentication needed via browser approval before end-to-end testing.

- All three requirements (reporting rows, exceptions lane for bad notes, audit script) documented in the brief.

- Claude Code applied a spawn fix; a scheduling review is now running to test the repair.
